summary: Backend + frontend regression of iter33 capacity-aware allocation display fix. Backend: 7/7 pytest tests still PASS — allocation_weekly_hours/compute_allocation_hours/my-allocations correctly respect standard_capacity. Frontend: formatAllocation h/wk display in allocation rows is FIXED (50%-cap resource at 10% now shows '10% (2.0h/wk)' — no more '4.0h/wk' bug). HOWEVER, a critical regression was found: the resource-group HEADER 'X% of capacity' still shows the RAW allocation percentage (10%) instead of the capacity-relative one (should be 20% = 10/50*100). Root cause: groupedByResource in Allocations.js (lines 190-201) builds the resource object from allocation data only and never propagates standard_capacity, so getCapacityPct falls back to cap=100 and returns rawPct unchanged. This same object is used by (a) resource header display, (b) summary stats (Available/AtCapacity/Over counts), and (c) getUtilBadge classification — so ALL THREE capacity-relative displays on the Weekly + By-Resource views are still wrong for part-time resources. Timeline view is CORRECT (uses resources list directly, which has standard_capacity).

ui_bugs.component: Allocations.js — renderResourceGroup header + Weekly summary stats
ui_bugs.issue: Resource-header 'X% of capacity' and Available/AtCapacity/Over counters still use raw allocation sum because groupedByResource never carries standard_capacity. For a 50%-cap resource with 10% raw alloc, header shows '10% of capacity' (should be 20%). For a 50%-cap resource with 50% raw alloc, header would show '50% of capacity' (should be 100%). Summary stat classification is likewise wrong for part-time resources.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Allocations.js lines 190-201, enrich resourceMap[rid] with standard_capacity from the resources list — e.g. `standard_capacity: (resources?.find(r => r.id === rid))?.standard_capacity || 100`. This will fix (a) getCapacityPct in the header, (b) the Available/AtCapacity/Over summary stats, and (c) getUtilBadge classification for part-time resources.
Optional: add data-testid attributes 'capacity-pct-{id}' to the resource header <span> that renders capacityPct to make future automated verification easier.
critical_code_review_comments: Allocations.js:190-201 — resourceMap does not include standard_capacity; the new getCapacityPct helper silently falls back to cap=100 for every resource, defeating the fix.
Allocations.js:344-352 — getTotalPercentage/getCapacityPct pair is correct, but only works when 'resource' has standard_capacity populated; currently only the timeline resources have it.

Allocations.js:468-470 — renderAllocationRow correctly does `resources?.find(r => r.id === alloc.resource_id)` to obtain standard_capacity for formatAllocation. Consistent pattern; consider using the same lookup in groupedByResource for a single fix.
capacityHelpers.js — formula (pct/100) * (cap/100) * 40 is applied everywhere; consider extracting HOURS_PER_WEEK=40 as a named export for clarity/testability.
getTotalPercentage sums percentages across overlapping date ranges without deduping by phase — acceptable for current 'flat' allocation model but flag for future when phase_allocations become richer.

success_rate.frontend: 62% (5/8 UI cases pass — h/wk row + backend hours + timeline+row+backend PASS; resource header capacity%, summary stats, and getUtilBadge classification for part-time resources FAIL)

test_credentials: admin@test.com / admin123 (super_admin). TEST_iter32_HalfCap (std_cap=50) reused from iter-32 seed data.
seed_data_creation: Created and DELETED one temporary allocation (10% percentage, Jul 27-31 2026) on TEST_iter32_HalfCap x Website Redesign, only for UI verification. Cleaned up at end of test. The TEST_iter32_HalfCap resource itself remains from iter-32 (was not cleaned up by iter-32 as claimed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: TEST_iter32_HalfCap resource (id 6a63664d3b426cd70594026d, std_cap=50) is present in DB. Use it + Website Redesign project (id 6a5d4ac2ad5ecf8fe70d55b2, wide date range 2026-01-01 to 2026-12-31) to create part-time allocations and verify capacity-relative displays. Current 'This Week' on server is 2026-07-20 to 2026-07-24; use next-week-btn or create allocations spanning current-day for immediate visibility in Weekly view. Bug reproduction: create any allocation for HalfCap, open Allocations Weekly view → resource header will show raw% instead of raw%/std_cap*100.

rca of the issue.bug: Resource-group header 'X% of capacity' and Available/AtCapacity/Over summary counters do NOT respect resource.standard_capacity as claimed by the iter-33 fix.
rca of the issue.reproduction: 1) Login admin@test.com/admin123. 2) POST /api/allocations for TEST_iter32_HalfCap (std_cap=50) with percentage=10. 3) Open Allocations → Weekly (navigate to the week containing the allocation). 4) Observe header shows '10% of capacity' (raw pct) instead of '20% of capacity' (= 10/50*100). Same object drives the summary counts and getUtilBadge, so classification is also raw-based.
rca of the issue.root_cause: Allocations.js `groupedByResource` (lines 187-220) builds resource objects from allocation rows only: `{id, name, role, allocations}`. Missing `standard_capacity`. `getCapacityPct(resource)` (lines 348-352) reads resource.standard_capacity → undefined → cap=100 fallback → returns rawPct unchanged. renderAllocationRow works because it independently looks up standard_capacity from the `resources` list (line 468-469).
rca of the issue.mitigation: In Allocations.js groupedByResource useMemo, enrich each resourceMap entry with `standard_capacity: (resources?.find(r => r.id === rid))?.standard_capacity ?? 100`. Add `resources` to the useMemo deps. Same fix would remediate summary stats and getUtilBadge classification for part-time resources.
